title: 缓存的基本概念
reason: 引入缓存概念，解释其位置和大小，是理解后续内容的基础。
review_question: 为什么CPU上要放置一个小容量的缓存？
transcript_evidence: One solution is to put a little piece of RAM right on the CPU -- called a cache. There isn’t a lot of space on a processor’s chip, so most caches are just kilobytes or maybe megabytes in size, where RAM is usually gigabytes.

title: 缓存加速的原理
reason: 说明缓存如何通过一次传输多个数据来加速，逻辑上承接clip_001。
review_question: 当CPU请求内存时，RAM如何传输数据以利用缓存？
transcript_evidence: Having a cache speeds things up in a clever way. When the CPU requests a memory location from RAM, the RAM can transmit not just one single value, but a whole block of data.

title: 数据块传输的实用性
reason: 解释数据块传输为何有用，因为数据通常顺序处理，与clip_002紧密相连。
review_question: 为什么传输一个数据块比只传输单个值更有效？
transcript_evidence: This takes only a little bit more time than transmitting a single value, but it allows this data block to be saved into the cache. This tends to be really useful because computer data is often arranged and processed sequentially.

title: 示例：餐厅销售总计
reason: 通过具体例子说明缓存如何工作，使抽象概念更易理解。
review_question: 在餐厅销售总计的例子中，处理器首先从哪个内存地址获取数据？
transcript_evidence: For example, let say the processor is totalling up daily sales for a restaurant. It starts by fetching the first transaction from RAM at memory location 100.

title: 缓存的速度优势
reason: 强调缓存靠近处理器带来的速度提升，与之前的概念形成对比。
review_question: 缓存能在多少个时钟周期内提供数据？
transcript_evidence: Because the cache is so close to the processor, it can typically provide the data in a single clock cycle -- no waiting required. This speeds things up tremendously over having to go back and forth to RAM every single time.

title: 缓存命中与未命中
reason: 定义关键术语“缓存命中”和“缓存未命中”，总结缓存工作原理。
review_question: 什么是缓存命中？什么是缓存未命中？
transcript_evidence: When data requested in RAM is already stored in the cache like this it’s called a cache hit, and if the data requested isn’t in the cache, so you have to go to RAM, it’s a called a cache miss.
